name: Hubble tension persistence
summary: Early-universe inferred H_0 from Planck CMB + LambdaCDM (67.4 +/- 0.5 km/s/Mpc) disagrees by ~5 sigma with late-universe direct measurement from Cepheid-calibrated Type Ia SNe (SH0ES: 73.04 +/- 1.04 km/s/Mpc; Riess et al. 2022). The tension has grown rather than shrunk over the past decade despite improvements in both methods, and is now the cleanest cosmological data anomaly. Either there is a systematic in one of the methods, or LambdaCDM is incomplete (early dark energy, modified recombination, varying constants, modified gravity).

characteristic_numbers.note: Riess et al. 2022, ApJL 934, L7, arXiv:2112.04510. Cepheid-calibrated SN Ia distance ladder.
After accounting for known systematics; varies 4-6 sigma depending on calibration method.

principles_stressed.note: If real, requires modifications to LambdaCDM at the few-percent level. Early dark energy (EDE) shifting the sound horizon at recombination is the leading new-physics candidate; modified recombination, dark radiation, varying fundamental constants, interacting dark sectors all proposed. None resolves the tension without introducing tensions elsewhere (S8, cluster counts, BAO).

history: Tension emerged in 2013 with Planck vs HST Cepheid measurements. Persistent and growing through 2017 (3 sigma), 2019 (4.4 sigma), 2022 (5 sigma). SH0ES 2022 (Riess et al. arXiv:2112.04510) is the highest-precision late-universe measurement. CMB-S4, JWST Cepheid recalibration, and Roman/Euclid will sharpen further.

references.citation: Riess et al. (SH0ES), 'A comprehensive measurement of the local value of the Hubble constant with 1 km/s/Mpc uncertainty from the Hubble Space Telescope and the SH0ES team', ApJL 934, L7 (2022)
Planck Collaboration, 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters', A&A 641, A6 (2020)
